free_text: Constraints on the dipole photon strength for the odd
uranium isotopes
Laboratory on Energy and Matter for Nuclear
Science Development, Ariana
INFN Sez. Perugia, Perugia; Univ. Perugia,
Perugia; Consiglio Nazionale delle Ricerche, Bari
CERN nTOF facility.
Protons were accelerated to 20 GeV/c.
Proton beam on lead spallation target
Highly enriched (>99%) uranium samples:
- 234U: 32.7 mg, 1.07E-04 atoms/b encapsulated in Ti,
pressed pellets of urnium oxide, 10 mm diameter
- 236U: 338 mg,  10.9E-04 atoms/b encapsulated in Al,
pressed pellets of urnium oxide, 10 mm diameter
- 238U: 6125 mg, 9.56E=04 atoms/b, metallic foil
encapsulated in Al (60 um) and Kapton (~75 um),
Total absorption calorimeter (TAC) consisting
of 40 BaF2 crystals in 4pi configuration with
crystal resolution on average 14.5% for 0.9 MeV
gamma-rays for 234U(n,g) and slightly worsened to
16.5% for 236U(n,g) and 238U(n,g)
Flight path length of ~185 m
Corrected for background.
Not corrected for dead time and pile-up, which were
minimized by selecting events in the resonance wings
where the count rate is small enough.
Statistical only
Data received from Javier Moreno-Soto.
ED. Data received from Javier Moreno-Soto.
